# PocketBase API Rules: Prevent Changing Owner

## Background

PocketBase uses SQL-like API rules to secure collection endpoints. You need to configure an Update API rule for a `posts` collection that ensures only the owner can update the record, and strictly prevents the `owner` field itself from being modified during the update.

## Requirements

* Initialize a PocketBase Go application in `/home/user/myproject`.
* Create a `posts` collection with at least the following fields:
  * `title` (type: text)
  * `owner` (type: relation, targeting the built-in `users` collection)
* Configure the **Update API rule** for the `posts` collection to enforce two conditions:
  1. The user performing the update must be the `owner` of the record.
  2. The `owner` field cannot be modified in the update request.

## Implementation Hints

* You can define the collection and rules using PocketBase's Go migrations (`pb_migrations`).
* In the API rule expression, use `@request.auth.id` to check the current user, and the `:isset` modifier on `@request.body` to ensure a field is not being changed.
* Make sure the application starts a standard PocketBase server on port 8090.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Start command: go run main.go serve --http="0.0.0.0:8090"
* Port: 8090
* Endpoints & Behavior:
  * The `posts` collection is accessible via the standard PocketBase REST API.
  * An authenticated user can update the `title` of a post they own.
  * An authenticated user receives a 400 or 403 error if they attempt to update the `owner` field of a post they own.
  * An authenticated user receives a 404 or 403 error if they attempt to update a post they do not own.
I'll start by planning this task and then implement it step by step.

todos.content: Create posts collection migration with title, owner fields and Update API rule
Verify the application builds and starts correctly

input.query: PocketBase Go API rule @request.auth.id @request.body isset modifier prevent field modification

content.text:
# Introduction - API rules and filters - Docs - PocketBase
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-11, [Read more](https://pocketbase.io/docs/api-rules-and-filters/)

- `@request.auth.*` - the current authenticated model (ex.
  `@request.auth.id != ""`)
...
##### :isset modifier
The `:isset` field modifier is available only for the `@request.*` fields and can be
used to check whether the client submitted a specific data with the request.
Here is for example a rule that
disallows submitting a "role" field:
`@request.body.role:isset = false`
...
##### :changed modifier
The `:changed` field modifier is available only for the `@request.body.*` fields and
can be used to check whether the client submitted AND changed a specific record field with the request.
Here
is for example a rule that disallows changing a "role" field:
`// the same as: (@request.body.role:isset = false || @request.body.role = role)
@request.body.role:changed = false`
...
### Examples
- Allow only registered users:
`@request.auth.id != ""`
- Allow only registered users and return records that are either "active" or "pending":
`@request.auth.id != "" && (status = "active" || status = "pending")`

# How to create an API rule which checks if the request user is valid.
created: 2022-12-25, last updated: 2026-04-17, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/1379)

```
// create
@request.data.author = @request.auth.id
// allow update only by the message author
author = @request.auth.id && (@request.data.author = "" || @request.data.author = @request.auth.id)
// allow delete only by the message author
author = @request.auth.id
```
Note that in the above **update** rule we also allow empty "author" data submission because the request is PATCH and if `author` is missing from the request body it will not update it (if the user explicitly set an empty id it will trigger the nonempty validation).
...
```
Note that in the above **update** rule we also allow empty "author" data submission because the request is PATCH and if `author` is missing from the request body it will not update it (if the user explicitly set an empty id it will trigger the nonempty validation).
In the upcoming v0.11 release this will be a little more clear since we'll have `isset` `@request.data.*` filter modifier and the update rule could look like this:
```
// allow update only by the message author and don't allow author change
author = @request.auth.id && @request.data.author:isset = false
```

# How to use API rules? · pocketbase pocketbase · Discussion #6573
created: 2025-03-08, last updated: 2026-03-21, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/6573)

For ex, if you have a field that you want the user to not change you can do:
```
@request.auth.id = user && @request.body.field:isset = false
```
This way, it fails if the user tries to update the `user` field.
You will need to add all your fields that you don't want to be changed with the :isset = false.
More about it here: https://pocketbase.io/docs/api-rules-and-filters/#isset-modifier
2 replies
...
`@request.body.*` is v0.23+ field.
From the "Set Admins only" button in the screenshot I assume you are using PocketBase <= v0.22.x (*you can find your version in the bottom-right corner*) and in that case you should use `@request.data.*`.

# Help debugging API Rule : r/pocketbase - Reddit
created: 2024-03-24, last updated: 2024-08-15, [Read more](https://www.reddit.com/r/pocketbase/comments/1bmodzy/help_debugging_api_rule/)

**organizations** collection, for **List** and **View:**
@request.auth.id != "" && (owners ?= @request.auth.id || members ?= @request.auth.id)
...
If I change the API Rule to:
@request.auth.id != "" && (owners:each = @request.auth.id || members:each @request.auth.id)
...
Change `owners` and `members` to `owners.id` and `members.id`.
Your rule is wrong because pb is comparing the value that is stored in the table, which for "many" relations is a json array, against your id.
...
The ":each" also should work (I've recently changed our code from `~` to it as it is more efficient) but you need "?" in front of the equal operators for any match (if you inspect the query you will see that the ":each" generates a SQLite json_each statement).
...
After you explain it - it makes sense.
The field is a relation field, and even tho the "value" stored in that field is the FK id itself, because its a relation, it needs to be addressed as such.

# Introduction - Authentication - Docs - PocketBase
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-15, [Read more](https://pocketbase.io/docs/authentication/)

Once you have obtained the **Client Id** and **Client Secret**, you can enable and configure the provider from your PocketBase auth collection options (*PocketBase > Collections > {YOUR_COLLECTION} > Edit collection (settings cogwheel) > Options
> OAuth2*).
...
PocketBase doesn't have a dedicated token verification endpoint, but if you want to verify an existing
auth token from a 3rd party app you can send an
Auth refresh call, aka.
`pb.collection("users").authRefresh()`.
On valid token - it returns a new token with refreshed `exp` claim and the latest user data.

# Prevent Role Change in PocketBase and Add Custom Fields to JWT · pocketbase pocketbase · Discussion #6577
created: 2025-03-09, last updated: 2026-04-17, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/6577)

To achieve this, I defined the following API rule for the update section:
**@request.auth.id = id && (@request.data.role:isset = false || @request.data.role = role)**
...
The API rules can prevent modifying a value but you can't prevent returning it in the response, or at least not easily in PocketBase <= v0.22.x.
You can attach to the specific request hooks and filter/modify the response from there but it is error prone an…The above API rules should work in the sense that it will prevent "role" field change.
Because of the `@request.data.*` field I assume you are using PocketBase <= v0.22.x.
If you are using PocketBase v0.23+ the field should be `@request.body.*`.
...
A better solution would be to move the "private" fields to a different collection that will have its own API rules and link back to the main one with a `relation` field.
...
- Go: https://pocketbase.io/docs/go-records/#hideunhide-fields
...
*PocketBase v0.23+ also added support for "hidden" fields, aka.
fields that can be changed only programmatically or by superusers.*

# API rules cheat sheet · pocketbase pocketbase · Discussion #3162
created: 2023-08-19, last updated: 2026-04-17, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/3162)

In general if a collection has for example a **non-empty "owner" user relation** field, then you can apply the following rules:
1. List/Search API rule - allow listing only the records that the current authenticated user owns
```
owner = @request.auth.id
```
2. View API rule - allow only owners
```
owner = @request.auth.id
```
3. Create API rule - require the submitted "owner" field to match the current authenticated user
```
@request.data.owner = @request.auth.id
```
> *Or alternatively, because the create API rule is applied after a dry submit, the below should also work:*
> ```
> owner = @request.auth.id
> ```
4. Update API rule - allow only owners AND prevent reassignment of the "owner" field
```
owner = @request.auth.id &&
@request.data.owner:isset = false
```
5. Delete API rule - allow only owners
```
owner = @request.auth.id
```
